extDesc.message: Bypass Runet censorship with PAC-based proxy rules.

providerAntizapretDescription.message: Main PAC script from the Antizapret project.

providerAnticensorityDescription.message: Alternative PAC script from the extension authors.

providerOnlyOwnSitesDescription.message: Use only manually configured site rules and proxy candidates.

popupPacProviderHelp.message: PAC decides which sites use proxy by default.

popupUpdatePacHelp.message: Download and rebuild PAC without applying proxy.

popupAutoModeHelp.message: Remove your custom rule. PAC decides.

popupProxyModeHelp.message: Force this site through Tor, WARP, or own proxy.

popupDirectModeHelp.message: Force this site to bypass proxy.

popupDomainScopeHelp.message: Apply the rule to the base domain and its subdomains, for example *.example.com.

popupSubdomainHeuristicNote.message: Domain scope uses the registrable domain and respects public suffix boundaries.

popupUsePacScriptProxiesHelp.message: Use proxies from selected PAC provider.

popupOwnProxiesOnlyForOwnSites.message: Own proxies only for own site rules

popupOwnProxiesOnlyForOwnSitesHelp.message: Use own proxies only for your site rules.

popupTorModeNote.message: Use one Tor mode at a time.

popupTorAvailabilityNote.message: Enabling Tor only makes it available for your Proxy rules. Auto mode still follows the PAC provider.

popupWarpHelp.message: Use a configured WARP or custom local proxy as a proxy candidate.

popupOwnProxiesHelp.message: Use proxies configured in Full settings. Passwords are hidden and used only for proxy authentication.

popupOwnProxiesConfigure.message: Own proxies: configure in full settings.

popupApplyChangesHelp.message: Save, rebuild PAC, and apply proxy.

popupClearProxyHelp.message: Disable this extension's browser proxy settings. Your rules and PAC cache are kept.

popupPacChanged.message: PAC settings changed. Apply changes to activate.

popupProxyCleared.message: Proxy cleared. Browser proxy mode returned to system.

optionsIntro.message: Manage PAC updates, site rules, proxy candidates, and browser proxy activation.

optionsLanguageReloadNote.message: The page will reload after changing language.

optionsQuickStartHint.message: After changing site or proxy rules, cook PAC again and apply it.

optionsPacChangedCookApply.message: PAC settings changed. Click Cook PAC, then Apply proxy.

optionsCookPacHelp.message: Rebuild PAC with your current rules.

optionsApplyProxyHelp.message: Activate the cooked PAC in browser proxy settings.

optionsPacCookedApplyNow.message: PAC cooked. You can apply proxy settings now.

optionsAddSiteRuleHint.message: Choose whether a site should use the proxy or open directly.

optionsToolbarTip.message: Tip: for the current tab, use the toolbar popup.

optionsProxyRuleNoCandidate.message: No proxy is enabled. Enable Tor, WARP, or an own proxy.

optionsLocalProxyHint.message: Enable Tor, WARP, or your own proxy before adding proxy rules.

optionsPasswordNotice.message: Passwords are saved only for proxy authentication and hidden after save.

optionsAdvancedPacRulesHint.message: Advanced rule changes also require Cook PAC, then Apply proxy.

optionsReplaceDirectWithProxy.message: Replace DIRECT with selected proxy candidates

optionsTorModeNote.message: Use one Tor mode at a time.

optionsLocalTorHelp.message: Use a separately running Tor daemon/service, usually SOCKS5 127.0.0.1:9050.

optionsTorBrowserHelp.message: Use the Tor proxy started by Tor Browser, usually SOCKS5 127.0.0.1:9150. Tor Browser must be open.

optionsPacProxyModeHelp.message: Choose whether provider PAC proxies are used and whether own proxies are limited to your site rules.

popupNoProxyCandidate.message: No proxy is enabled. Enable Tor, WARP, or an own proxy.

popupPeriodicUpdateRunning.message: Periodic PAC update is already running.

popupPacOperationRunning.message: Another PAC operation is already running.

popupMv2RulesWarning.message: This advanced legacy rule is listed under About / Limitations.

popupCredentialsRedactedWarning.message: Proxy passwords are hidden in PAC and used only for authentication.

popupWarpLocalProxyWarning.message: WARP is treated as a local proxy.

popupDirectReplacementNoCandidateWarning.message: DIRECT replacement needs an enabled proxy candidate.

popupNoPacOrOwnProxyWarning.message: PAC provider proxies are disabled and no own proxy is enabled.

optionsDiagnosticsHint.message: Technical state, previews, periodic update, and auth logs.

optionsMaintenanceHint.message: Use this only if you upgraded from the old MV2 version and want to copy old settings. This does not delete old data and does not apply proxy settings.

optionsMv2RulesWarning.message: This advanced legacy rule is listed under About / Limitations.

optionsAboutHint.message: Version details, credits, and non-intrusive compatibility notes.

optionsLimitationDns.message: Not restored: DNS monkey-patching / DNS leak PAC overrides. Disabled for safety and predictability.

optionsLimitationRawReplaceDirect.message: Not restored: arbitrary raw replaceDirectWith PAC strings. Disabled to avoid injecting executable PAC code.

optionsLimitationSecureFiltering.message: Not restored for RC: secure-provider-proxy filtering and HTTPS-only proxying.

optionsLimitationWeightedRules.message: Partial: full legacy weighted exception edge cases are not restored.

optionsLimitationExternalProxyQa.message: Pending QA: external authenticated proxy and HTTPS CONNECT.

optionsLimitationTorQa.message: Pending QA: real Tor traffic requires Tor installed and running.

optionsRestoredPacProxySwitch.message: PAC provider proxy switch is restored: provider proxies can be enabled or disabled.

optionsRestoredOwnSitesOnly.message: Own proxies only for own site rules is restored and enabled by default.

optionsRestoredOnionTor.message: .onion routing through enabled Tor candidates is restored.

optionsRestoredDirectPolicy.message: DIRECT replacement and no-DIRECT policy are available as advanced opt-in settings.

optionsCredentialsRedactedWarning.message: Proxy passwords are hidden in PAC and used only for authentication.

optionsWarpLocalProxyWarning.message: WARP is treated as a local proxy.

optionsDirectReplacementNoCandidateWarni.message: DIRECT replacement needs an enabled proxy candidate.

optionsNoPacOrOwnProxyWarning.message: PAC provider proxies are disabled and no own proxy is enabled.

optionsEnterSite.message: Enter a site, domain, or URL.

optionsEnterValidSite.message: Enter a valid domain, host, wildcard, or URL.

optionsDuplicateSiteRule.message: This site already has a rule.

optionsPeriodicUpdatesHint.message: The selected PAC provider is checked every hour and refreshed when 12 hours have elapsed. A newly cooked PAC is applied only when this extension already controls proxy settings.

optionsPeriodicAuthHint.message: External authenticated proxy QA is still pending.

optionsLegacyMigrationHint.message: Use this only if you upgraded from the old MV2 version and want to copy old settings. This does not delete old data and does not apply proxy settings.

optionsSettingsCannotMigrateSafely.message: Settings that cannot be migrated safely

optionsMigrationConfirmation.message: I understand this will copy selected legacy MV2 settings into MV3 settings. It will not delete old MV2 data and will not apply proxy settings.

optionsPacProvidersHint.message: Built-in providers are read-only. Add trusted custom PAC URLs when you need another provider.

optionsPacUrlsHint.message: One URL per line. Use HTTPS, or localhost HTTP for local testing.

optionsProviderDuplicateUrls.message: Duplicate URLs are removed automatically. Only add PAC URLs you trust.

optionsCustomProviderAdded.message: Custom PAC provider added. Select it, then update PAC.

optionsCustomProviderUpdatedRefresh.message: Provider URLs changed. Update PAC, cook it, and apply proxy again.

optionsCustomProviderDeleted.message: Custom PAC provider deleted. Cached artifacts were kept.

optionsSelectedProviderCleared.message: The selected custom provider was disabled. Select another provider. An already applied proxy remains active until you clear or replace it.

optionsSelectedProviderDeleted.message: The selected custom provider was deleted. Select another provider. Cached artifacts were kept; an already applied proxy remains active until you clear or replace it.

optionsProviderUrlRequired.message: Add at least one PAC URL.

optionsProviderInvalidUrl.message: One of the PAC URLs is invalid.

optionsProviderInvalidScheme.message: PAC URLs must use HTTPS. Localhost HTTP is allowed for testing.

optionsProviderUrlCredentials.message: PAC URLs must not contain a username or password.

optionsProviderTooManyUrls.message: A custom provider may contain at most 10 PAC URLs.

optionsProviderNotFound.message: Custom PAC provider was not found.

optionsProviderBuiltInReadOnly.message: Built-in PAC providers cannot be edited or deleted.

popupProxyCheckRequiresProxyRule.message: Enable Proxy mode for the current site before checking.

popupProxyCheckInconclusive.message: The check was inconclusive. No proxy-specific browser error was reported.

proxyHealthTorBrowserError.message: Could not connect to Tor Browser. Make sure Tor Browser is running and 127.0.0.1:9150 is available.

proxyHealthLocalTorError.message: Could not connect to the Tor service. Make sure Tor is running and 127.0.0.1:9050 is available.

proxyHealthGenericError.message: Could not connect through the configured proxy.

proxyHealthTorBrowserNotification.message: Tor Browser is unavailable. Start Tor Browser and try again.

proxyHealthLocalTorNotification.message: The Tor service is unavailable. Start Tor and try again.

optionsReliabilityHint.message: PAC refresh and proxy connectivity status. Automatic refresh never enables a cleared proxy.

periodicUpdateNotificationBody.message: The automatic PAC update failed. Open settings for details.
